summary: Stop chasing the "best mutual fund" and start building real wealth. In this episode of This or That with Bhartendra Singh, I sit down with Krishnan Sharma, a veteran financial educator with 30+ years of experience, to reveal why your behavior matters far more than your portfolio selection.

seo.title: How to Become a Good Investor | Why the “Best Fund” Does Not Exist
seo.description: Stop chasing the "best mutual fund" and start building real wealth. In this episode of This or That with Bhartendra Singh, I sit down with Krishnan

takeaways.text: The best fund is the wrong question to start with. This episode argues your behavior with money matters far more than which scheme you pick, so building a clear philosophy beats hunting for a winner.
Switching funds because last year's chart looked good is treated as a trap, not a strategy. Chasing recent performance is exactly the habit the conversation asks you to drop.
Making money and creating wealth are not the same thing. High returns do not automatically buy financial freedom, which is why the episode separates income from real wealth creation.
The harder work is detachment when markets get loud. Writing down your own investment philosophy is offered as a practical anchor for holding steady through fear and greed.
A good advisor is there to manage your behavior, not just your money, and structuring a portfolio with an 80/20 strategic-versus-tactical split is framed as a route to peace of mind.
